author: George Orwell
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "1984" book by George Orwell?
Options:
A. However, meeting in person was extremely challenging. It felt hopeless, like attempting a chess move when you're already checkmated. No matter which way you looked, the telescreen faced you. In fact, he had considered every potential way to communicate with her within 5 minutes of reading the note. But now, as time passed, he went over each option again carefully, as if organizing tools on a table. Clearly, what had transpired that morning could not happen again. If she worked in Records it may have been relatively simple, but he only vaguely knew where Fiction was, and had no justification for going there.
B. However, it was extremely challenging for them to meet in person. It felt impossible, like trying to make a chess move when you've already been checkmated. No matter which direction you faced, the telescreen was watching you. In fact, within 5 minutes of reading her note, he had already thought of all the potential ways he could communicate with her. But now, as time passed, he methodically considered each option again, as if carefully laying out tools on a table. Clearly, what had happened that morning could not happen again. If she had worked in Records it might have been fairly simple, but he only had a vague sense of where Fiction was located, and no excuse to go there.
C. But actually meeting face-to-face was tremendously difficult. It was as futile as trying to make a chess move when you're already beaten. The telescreen was staring at you whichever way you turned. He had already thought of every possible way to contact her within 5 minutes of reading her note. But now, as time went on, he reviewed each idea again, one by one, like placing instruments on a table. Obviously they couldn't repeat what had happened that morning. If she had been in Records it might have been fairly easy, but he barely knew where Fiction was, and had no reason to go there.  
D. But the physical difficulty of meeting was enormous. It was like trying to make a move at chess when you were already mated. Whichever way you turned, the telescreen faced you. Actually, all the possible ways of communicating with her had occurred to him within five minutes of reading the note; but now, with time to think, he went over them one by one, as though laying out a row of instruments on a table. Obviously the kind of encounter that had happened this morning could not be repeated. If she had worked in the Records Department it might have been comparatively simple, but he had only a very dim idea whereabouts in the building the Fiction Department lay, and he had no pretext for going there.
Answer:

options.A: However, meeting in person was extremely challenging. It felt hopeless, like attempting a chess move when you're already checkmated. No matter which way you looked, the telescreen faced you. In fact, he had considered every potential way to communicate with her within 5 minutes of reading the note.
